Career Highlights

Quick Facts
  • Son of Robert and Christine Goodpaster
  • Has six siblings, Benjamin, David, John, Maria, Michael and Rachel
  • Began skating at age 12, and skating with figure skating partner, Sarah Rose, in 2014
  • Grew up on a farm in Pennsylvania and raised sheep, goats, chickens, rabbits, and ducks
  • Hobbies include swimming, baking, and playing ping pong and tennis
  • Volunteers at Knights Helping Knights Food Pantry and teaches Learn to Skate classes with Rose.

Quick Facts
  • Son of John and Eva
  • One of five children
  • Married to Ashley DeAtley
  • Discovered the sport of handball after seeing it on television during the Beijing 2008 Olympic Games
  • Played with the Los Angeles Team Handball Club for five years
  • Spent a season training and playing with Sg Nussloch Handball Club in Nussloch, Germany
  • Trained full-time as part of the USA Team Handball Residency Program in Auburn, Alabama
  • Moved back to California in May of 2018
  • Is a big sports fan, specifically a fan of the Dodgers, Kings, Lakers and Steelers.
Career Highlights
  • 2019 So. Cal Beach Handball Championships - 1st, Most Valuable Goalkeeper

  • 2014 Pan American Championships - 6th (Canelones, Uruguay)
World Championship Experience
  • 2018 IHF Beach Handball World Championships - 12th (Kazan, Russia)

Quick Facts

  • Competed in wrestling, track and field and football in high school, but excelled in wrestling where he was named the team MVP as a senior, totaling a 35-3 record that season.

  • Introduced to judo through a physical education class at the University of Minnesota as a freshman in 2011.

  • Earned degrees in finance and accounting from the University of Minnesota, worked as a sales tax technician for the City of Colorado Springs while training for the Olympic Games Tokyo 2020.

  • Made his first international team in 2013

  • Resides in Sweden where he trains along with his wife, Nicolina Pernheim.
Paralympic Experience
  • 2-time Paralympian; 1-time Paralympic medalist (1 silver)
    • Paralympic Games Tokyo 2020, silver (100kg - Men)
    • Paralympic Games Rio de Janeiro 2016, 9th (+100kg - Men)
Career Highlights
  • Parapan American Games Lima 2019, silver medal
  • Three-time Pan American Championships medalist
  • Three-time Judo Grand Prix medalist
  • 2013 Youth World Championships, gold medal

Marcus Gore was born in Vail and grew up ski racing in the Ski & Snowboard Club Vail program. At the age of 14, he decided to pursue cross-country skiing instead.


He discovered biathlon in the same way as many young nordic skiers in the West, through the West Yellowstone biathlon clinics hosted by Marc Sheppard. He never gets tired of hearing targets drop. Now as part of the team, Marcus has high hopes and big goals for the coming seasons.

Career Highlights

  • 2014 IBAF Women's Baseball World Cup, 2nd

  • Daughter of Gilbert Gortarez
  • Hobbies include running and playing basketball.

  • In high school, he also competed in track and field
  • Drafted by the Philadelphia Phillies in the 2nd round of the 2008 MLB June Amateur Draft
  • Previously played for the Toronto Blue Jays and the Detroit Tigers
  • Changed positions from center fielder to pitcher following the 2016 season

  • 2009 SAL Post-Season All-Star
Olympic Experience

    • Olympic Games Tokyo 2020, silver (Team - Men)

NGB Bio

bio.education: Rolesville High School (Wake Forest, N.C.) University of Texas at Arlington ‘09, Kinesiology
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Gouge started playing wheelchair basketball when he was 11 with a local team in Raleigh, North Carolina. After a couple years, he started playing competitively for the Charlotte Hornets. He went on to play at the University of Texas at Arlington. Following college, he played professionally in France for two years
  • Son of Roger and Nancy Gouge
  • Has one older sister, Amber
  • Married to Amber Gouge
  • In 2007, the Mayor of Arlington officially declared November 2 as “Aaron Gouge Day”
  • Used to compete in track and field and weightlifting where he broke several junior weightlifting records
  • Hopes to get into coaching
  • Favorite college basketball team is the North Carolina Tar Heels
  • Enjoys hanging out with friends, watching movies and sports, and traveling.
Paralympic Experience
  • 1-time Paralympian; 1-time Paralympic medalist (1 gold)
    • Paralympic Games Rio de Janeiro 2016, gold (Team - Men)
World Championship Experience
  • Most recent: 2018 – silver (Team - Men)
  • Years of participation: Team - Men 2014, 2018
  • Medals: 2 (2 silver)
    • Silver – 2018 (Team - Men); 2014 (Team - Men)

Quick Facts
  • Wears No. 31
  • Parents are David and Denise Gourley
  • Personal motto: Be where your feet are. Live and embrace each moment you're in and what you get to experience at that very second
  • Three words to describe her are: friendly, easy going and optimistic
  • If she wasn't playing softball she would be: If I wasn't playing softball I'd be living at the beach and surfing much better than I surfed when I attempted to the first time!
  • On what it means to represent the United States in softball: "Having the opportunity to represent the US is such an honor. I am so grateful and thankful to play for the best country in the world. I cant thank the sport of softball enough for giving me chances like this."
Career Highlights
  • World Cup, Oklahoma City, 2017 (Team - Women)
  • World Cup, Oklahoma City, 2016 (Team - Women)
World Championship Experience
  • Most recent: 2016 – gold (Team - Women)
  • Years of participation: Team - Women 2016
  • Medals: 1 (1 gold)
    • Gold – 2016 (Team - Women)
More

COLLEGIATE HONORS

2016: NFCA All-American ...USA Softball Player of the Year Top 10 Finalist...First Team All-SEC

2015: NCAA WCWS Champion...Tossed a scoreless seventh to earn the save in Game 1 of the WCWS Championship Series against Michigan

2014: NCAA WCWS Champion
